meta.title: Vito W447 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes Vito W447 engine — M274, OM622, OM651, OM654. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the OM651 chain risk explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes Vito W447 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned W447 engine typically costs £1,800–£6,000 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], depending on engine — the OM622 1.6/1.7 diesel is the cheapest at £1,800–£3,500, while the OM654 2.0 diesel runs £3,000–£6,000. Fitting adds £650–£910. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes Vito W447 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M274 2.0‑litre petrol (Vito 200) is the most reliable choice — it has no timing‑chain weakness and its cooling‑system issues are well‑understood. Among diesels, the OM622 is simple and economical, while the OM651 and OM654 need careful maintenance — chain monitoring for the OM651, AdBlue upkeep for the OM654.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes Vito W447?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM651 diesels (pre‑2020) have a rear‑mounted timing chain that can stretch; OM654 diesels (post‑2019) can suffer injector wear and AdBlue system issues; OM622 front‑drive diesels can develop injector wear and EGR clogging; and M274 petrols can suffer water pump leaks. Regular servicing is critical across all engines.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes Vito W447 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine and service history. The OM651 is a proven workhorse but chain monitoring is essential. The OM654 is more modern but AdBlue complexity is emerging. The OM622 is simple and robust for front‑drive applications. The M274 petrol is the most reliable but thirstier. Overall, a well‑maintained W447 can be a dependable commercial vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes Vito W447 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright "avoid" — but a pre‑2020 OM651 without documented chain replacement should be approached with caution. A high‑mileage OM654 with unresolved AdBlue issues also warrants careful inspection. The OM622 is the safe bet for front‑drive buyers who prioritise simplicity.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my Vito W447 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs include: cold‑start rattle (OM651 chain), rough idle or AdBlue warnings (OM654), rough idle or loss of power (OM622 injectors), or coolant loss with no visible leak (M274 water pump). If you experience any of these, get a diagnosis immediately — early intervention saves thousands.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes Vito W447 engine, or should I scrap the van?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the van's value and condition. Against a well‑kept £8,000 Vito, a £3,500 OM651 recon is 44% of the van's value — safe if the van is otherwise sound. Against a tired £3,500 example, the same recon is 100% — walk away. Use our 70% rule as your guide.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes Vito W447 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, the OM651 can exceed 200,000 miles with careful chain monitoring; the OM654 is capable of similar mileage with AdBlue system upkeep; the OM622 can reach 200,000+ miles with regular injector maintenance; the M274 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the pre‑facelift and facelift W447?

acceptedAnswer.text: The pre‑facelift (2014–2020) used the OM651 2.1‑litre diesel on rear‑drive models and the OM622 1.6‑litre diesel on front‑drive models. The 2020 facelift replaced the OM651 with the OM654 2.0‑litre diesel, enlarged the OM622 to 1.7 litres, and introduced the M274 petrol option. The facelift also brought exterior styling and interior tech updates.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes Vito W447 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible on the door pillar) and in the vehicle documentation. The code is stamped on the engine block — on OM651/OM654 it's on the front left, on OM622 it's on the front right, and on M274 it's on the right side. Tell us your variant and registration year, and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: Mercedes Vito W447 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes Vito W447 Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: OM651 and OM654 ratings: 95% confidence, inherited from the Model Page where one exists. OM622 and M274: Expert‑knowledge tier, derived from cross‑referencing specialist and owner sources — confirm against the Model Page before publishing.

overview.intro: The W447 is the third generation of the Mercedes‑Benz Vito, a light commercial vehicle produced from 2014. It introduced front‑wheel drive to the Vito range for the first time alongside traditional rear‑wheel drive, with a 1.6‑litre diesel powering front‑drive models. The range includes panel van, Tourer minibus (up to nine seats), and Mixto (two rows of seats with a cargo area) configurations. A significant facelift in March 2020 brought engine changes: the 2.1‑litre OM651 was replaced by the 2.0‑litre OM654 on rear‑ and all‑wheel‑drive models, while front‑drive models received an enlarged 1.7‑litre version of the OM622.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz W447 • Predecessor: W639 • Successor: Current (facelift ongoing) • Sibling body styles in this generation: Panel van, Tourer minibus, Mixto.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No W447‑specific enquiry figure is available; OM651 diesel enquiries dominate the broader Mercedes van parc, with 1,250 UK requests in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.ownershipVerdict: Budget Diesel — cheap to run, cheap to fix
Watch the Chain — rear‑mounted chain is the key risk
Modern Diesel — efficient, but AdBlue adds complexity

economicsRule.title: The W447 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,500–£5,500 OM651 recon against a £4,000–£12,000 Vito sits at 21–138% of the van's value. On a sub‑£4,000 example, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well‑kept £8,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 31–69% — a repair decision, not a financial one. The OM622 is the exception — its low recon cost often makes replacement viable even on lower‑value vans.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W447 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM622 1.6/1.7 diesel — cheapest recon, simple maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W447 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM654 2.0 diesel — injectors and AdBlue system add complexity.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W447 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: Post‑2020 OM654 136 PS — modern efficiency with proven durability.

problems.title: OM651 Timing‑Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.affectedModels: W447 114 CDI, 116 CDI, 119 CDI / BlueTEC (pre‑2020 OM651 diesel).
problems.rootCause: The OM651 has a rear‑mounted timing chain that can stretch over time. Cold‑start rattle is the classic early warning — if ignored, the chain can jump and cause catastrophic engine damage.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle on an OM651, get the chain inspected immediately — a £600–£1,500 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows if ignored. On a Vito worth under £4,000, this decision alone determines whether the van stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare OM651 engine prices for your Vito W447 →

problems.title: OM654 Injector & AdBlue System Issues
problems.affectedModels: W447 114 CDI, 116 CDI, 119 CDI, 200 CDI, 220 CDI, 250 CDI, 300 CDI (post‑2019 OM654 diesel).
problems.rootCause: The OM654 common‑rail injectors can fail, leading to rough running and excessive smoke. The AdBlue (SCR) system — required for Euro 6d‑TEMP compliance — can also develop sensor and heater issues, triggering warning lights and potential limp mode.
problems.recommendation: If your OM654‑powered Vito develops a rough idle or an AdBlue warning light, have it diagnosed immediately — a £500–£1,200 repair is far cheaper than the £3,000+ replacement that follows a full failure. Regular AdBlue system maintenance is essential.

cta.label: Compare OM654 engine prices for your Vito W447 →

problems.title: OM622 Injector & EGR Wear
problems.rootCause: The OM622 (Renault‑sourced) diesel can develop injector wear and EGR clogging, particularly if used for short trips or with poor‑quality fuel. Rough idle and loss of power are common symptoms.
problems.recommendation: If your front‑drive Vito develops a rough idle or hesitation, have the injectors and EGR system checked — a £300–£800 repair is far cheaper than the £1,800+ replacement that follows a full failure.

cta.label: Compare OM622 engine prices for your Vito W447 →

problems.title: M274 Water Pump & Cooling System Leaks
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump housing and coolant crossover pipes on the M274 can crack with age and heat cycling, leading to gradual coolant loss and potential overheating.
problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £300–£600 water pump replacement is cheap insurance against a £1,500+ head‑gasket repair if the engine overheats.

cta.label: Compare M274 engine prices for your Vito W447 →

replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Model Page's baseline figures — W447 shares its engine bay architecture across body styles, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required. OM651 and OM654 costs carried forward from the EM data's Table A.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are W447 across all body styles (panel van, Tourer, Mixto). The W447 chassis code covers the full range regardless of configuration.
